title: Divergence In Chinas Coal Output And Coal Derived Electricity Generation Continues
section_path: Divergence In Chinas Coal Output And Coal Derived Electricity Generation Continues
section_path_text: Divergence In Chinas Coal Output And Coal Derived Electricity Generation Continues
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
As we discussed in Commodore Research's most recent Weekly China Report, China's coal output totaled 390.4 million tons in July. This is down month-on-month by 15 million tons (-4%) but up year-on-year by 12.9 million tons (3%). This is a strong level of production (the strongest ever for the month of July) and has marked a second straight month where China's coal production has grown on a year-on-year basis.
